CREATE TABLE urls (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, scheme VARCHAR(10), authority VARCHAR(255), path VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO urls (scheme, authority, path) VALUES ('https', 'usr@www.a.com:123', 'docs/index.html');
INSERT INTO urls (scheme, authority, path) VALUES ('http', 'guest@www.test.com', 'home/overview');
INSERT INTO urls (scheme, authority, path) VALUES ('ftp', 'ftp.test.org', 'files/data.txt');
INSERT INTO urls (scheme, authority, path) VALUES ('mailto', 'info@example.com', 'contact');
INSERT INTO urls (scheme, authority, path) VALUES ('https', 'admin@secure-site.org:443', 'private/docs');
SELECT sys.newurl(scheme, authority, path) FROM urls WHERE id = 1;
SELECT sys.newurl(scheme, authority, path) FROM urls WHERE scheme IN ('https', 'http');
SELECT sys.newurl(scheme, authority, path) FROM urls WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM urls WHERE scheme = 'ftp');
SELECT a.id, sys.newurl(a.scheme, a.authority, a.path) AS generated_url FROM urls a JOIN urls b ON a.scheme = b.scheme WHERE a.id <> b.id;
CREATE TABLE test_urls AS SELECT sys.newurl(scheme, authority, path) AS full_url FROM urls;
SELECT * FROM test_urls WHERE full_url LIKE 'https://%';
CREATE VIEW url_view AS SELECT scheme, authority, path, sys.newurl(scheme, authority, path) AS full_url FROM urls;
SELECT * FROM url_view WHERE authority LIKE '%test.com';
INSERT INTO urls (scheme, authority, path) VALUES ('https', '', 'docs/index.html');
